(A) read as follows: “Beginning with the calendar quarter ending , and quarterly thereafter, the Secretary shall determine the amount of all taxes imposed by, and collected during the quarter under, the internal revenue laws of the United States on articles produced in the Virgin Islands and transported to the United States. The amount so determined less 1 percent and less the estimated amount of refunds or credits shall be subject to disposition as follows:Pub. L. 106–200, § 602(b)September 30, 1975“(A) There shall be transferred and paid over, as soon as practicable after the close of the quarter, to the Government of the Virgin Islands from the amounts so determined a sum equal to the total amount of the revenue collected by the Government of the Virgin Islands during the quarter, as certified by the Government Comptroller of the Virgin Islands. Payment to Puerto Rico or Virgin Islands of Amounts With Respect to Medicines, etc. Unfit for Beverage Purposes, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–514, title XVIII, § 1879(i)(3)100 Stat. 2907Oct. 22, 1986“(A)Section 7652 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 [now 1986] (other than subsection (f) thereof) shall not prevent the payment to Puerto Rico or the Virgin Islands of amounts with respect to medicines, medicinal preparations, food products, flavors, or flavoring extracts containing distilled spirits, which are unfit for beverage purposes and which are brought into the United States from Puerto Rico or the Virgin Islands on or before the date of the enactment of this Act [].Oct. 22, 1986“(B)With respect to articles brought into the United States after , subparagraph (A) shall apply only if the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate is satisfied that the amounts paid to Puerto Rico or the Virgin Islands under subparagraph (A) are being repaid to the proper persons who used the distilled spirits in such articles.”September 27, 1985
Executive Documents

Ex. Ord. No. 10602. Secretary of the Interior as Representative of PresidentEx. Ord. No. 10602, , 20 F.R. 1795, provided: “By virtue of the authority vested in me by section 7652(b)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 [now I.R.C. 1986] (Public Law 591, 83rd Congress, ), I hereby designate the Secretary of the Interior as the representative of the President to approve the obligation and expenditure by the government of the Virgin Islands of the moneys referred to in the said section 7652(b)(3).”Mar. 24, 195568A Stat. 907[ made various amendments to subsec. (b)(3) of this section, after which there was no longer the requirement for approval by the President or his designated representative of obligation and expenditure of specified moneys.]Pub. L. 94–455Dwight D. Eisenhower

No. 10602, , 20 F.R. 1795, provided: “By virtue of the authority vested in me by section 7652(b)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 [now I.R.C. 1986] (Public Law 591, 83rd Congress, ), I hereby designate the Secretary of the Interior as the representative of the President to approve the obligation and expenditure by the government of the Virgin Islands of the moneys referred to in the said section 7652(b)(3).”Mar. 24, 195568A Stat. 907[ made various amendments to subsec. (b)(3) of this section, after which there was no longer the requirement for approval by the President or his designated representative of obligation and expenditure of specified moneys.]Pub. L. 94–455Dwight D. Eisenhower

children.heading: Rate of tax
children.label: 1 Rate of tax
children.text: Except as provided in section 5314, articles of merchandise of Puerto Rican manufacture coming into the United States and withdrawn for consumption or sale shall be subject to a tax equal to the internal revenue tax imposed in the United States upon the like articles of merchandise of domestic manufacture.

children.heading: Payment of tax
children.label: 2 Payment of tax
children.text: The Secretary shall by regulations prescribe the mode and time for payment and collection of the tax described in paragraph (1), including any discretionary method described in section 6302(b) and (c). Such regulations shall authorize the payment of such tax before shipment from Puerto Rico, and the provisions of section 7651(2)(B) shall be applicable to the payment and collection of such tax in Puerto Rico.

children.heading: Deposit of internal revenue collections
children.label: 3 Deposit of internal revenue collections
children.text: All taxes collected under the internal revenue laws of the United States on articles produced in Puerto Rico and transported to the United States (less the estimated amount necessary for payment of refunds and drawbacks), or consumed in the island, shall be covered into the treasury of Puerto Rico.

children.heading: Taxes imposed in the United States
children.label: 1 Taxes imposed in the United States
children.text: Except as provided in section 5314, there shall be imposed in the United States, upon articles coming into the United States from the Virgin Islands, a tax equal to the internal revenue tax imposed in the United States upon like articles of domestic manufacture.

children.heading: Exemption from tax imposed in the Virgin Islands
children.label: 2 Exemption from tax imposed in the Virgin Islands
children.text: Such articles shipped from such islands to the United States shall be exempt from the payment of any tax imposed by the internal revenue laws of such islands.

children.label: A
children.text: The payment of an estimated amount shall be made to the government of the Virgin Islands before the commencement of each fiscal year as set forth in section 4(c)(2) of the Act entitled “An Act to authorize appropriations for certain insular areas of the United States, and for other purposes”, approved (), as in effect on the date of the enactment of the Trade and Development Act of 2000. The payment so made shall constitute a separate fund in the treasury of the Virgin Islands and may be expended as the legislature may determine.August 18, 197848 U.S.C. 1645

children.label: B
children.text: Any amounts remaining shall be deposited in the Treasury of the United States as miscellaneous receipts.

children.heading: Articles containing distilled spirits
children.label: c Articles containing distilled spirits
children.text: For purposes of subsections (a)(3) and (b)(3), any article containing distilled spirits shall in no event be treated as produced in Puerto Rico or the Virgin Islands unless at least 92 percent of the alcoholic content in such article is attributable to rum.

children.heading: Articles other than articles containing distilled spirits
children.label: d Articles other than articles containing distilled spirits

children.heading: Value added requirement for Puerto Rico
children.label: 1 Value added requirement for Puerto Rico
A
children.text: the cost or value of the materials produced in Puerto Rico, plus

children.label: B
children.text: the direct costs of processing operations performed in Puerto Rico,

children.heading: Prohibition of Federal excise tax subsidies
children.label: 2 Prohibition of Federal excise tax subsidies

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No amount shall be transferred under subsection (a)(3) or (b)(3) in respect of taxes imposed on any article, other than an article containing distilled spirits, if the Secretary determines that a Federal excise tax subsidy was provided by Puerto Rico or the Virgin Islands (as the case may be) with respect to such article.

children.label: i
children.text: of a kind different from, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in an amount per value or volume of production greater than,

children.heading: Direct costs of processing operations
children.label: 3 Direct costs of processing operations
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “direct cost of processing operations” has the same meaning as when used in section 213 of the Caribbean Basin Economic Recovery Act.

children.heading: Shipments of rum to the United States
children.label: e Shipments of rum to the United States

children.heading: Excise taxes on rum covered into treasuries of Puerto Rico and Virgin Islands
children.label: 1 Excise taxes on rum covered into treasuries of Puerto Rico and Virgin Islands
children.text: All taxes collected under section 5001(a)(1) on rum imported into the United States (less the estimated amount necessary for payment of refunds and drawbacks) shall be covered into the treasuries of Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands.

children.heading: Secretary prescribes formula
children.label: 2 Secretary prescribes formula
children.text: The Secretary shall, from time to time, prescribe by regulation a formula for the division of such tax collections between Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands and the timing and methods for transferring such tax collections.

children.heading: Rum defined
children.label: 3 Rum defined
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “rum” means any article classified under subheading 2208.40.00 of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States ().19 U.S.C. 1202

children.heading: Coordination with subsections (a) and (b)
children.label: 4 Coordination with subsections (a) and (b)
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply with respect to any rum subject to tax under subsection (a) or (b).

children.heading: Limitation on cover over of tax on distilled spirits
children.label: f Limitation on cover over of tax on distilled spirits

children.text: the tax imposed under subsection (a)(1) of section 5001, determined as if subsection (c)(1) of such section did not apply, on each proof gallon.

children.heading: Drawback for medicinal alcohol, etc.
children.label: g Drawback for medicinal alcohol, etc.
A
children.text: the use and tax determination described in section 5111 had occurred in the United States by a United States person at the time the article is brought into the United States, and

children.label: B
children.text: the rate of tax were the rate applicable under subsection (f) of this section, and
no amount shall be covered into the treasuries of Puerto Rico or the Virgin Islands.

children.heading: Manner of cover over of tax must be derived from this title
children.label: h Manner of cover over of tax must be derived from this title

children.text: any provision of law which is not contained in this title or in a revenue Act; and
whether such provision of law is a subsequently enacted provision or directly or indirectly seeks to waive the application of this subsection.

children.heading: Determination of taxes collected
children.label: i Determination of taxes collected
children.text: For purposes of subsections (a)(3), (b)(3), and (e)(1), refunds under section 5001(c)(4) shall not be taken into account as a refund, and the amount of taxes imposed by and collected under section 5001(a)(1) shall be determined without regard to section 5001(c).
